title: How Alessio Fanelli uses Open AI Symphony for Autonomous Coding and Pokémon Card Trading Workflows | ChatPRD Blog
author: Claire Vo
excerpt: Alessio Fanelli, founder of Kernel Labs and co-host of the Latent Space podcast, reveals two powerful AI workflows: managing autonomous coding agents with OpenAI Symphony and Linear, and using Codex to find and buy underpriced Pokémon cards on eBay.
body_excerpt: How Alessio Fanelli uses Open AI Symphony for Autonomous Coding and Pokémon Card Trading Workflows | ChatPRD Blog ChatPRD Open menu Product Enterprise Customers Pricing Resources Reviews How I AI Log in Get a demo Start free ← All articles Back / How I AI / How Alessio Fanelli uses Open AI Symphony for Autonomous Coding and Pokémon Card Trading Workflows How I AI How Alessio Fanelli uses Open AI Symphony for Autonomous Coding and Pokémon Card Trading Workflows Alessio Fanelli, founder of Kernel Labs and co-host of the Latent Space podcast, reveals two powerful AI workflows: managing autonomous coding agents with OpenAI Symphony and Linear, and using Codex to find and buy underpriced Pokémon cards on eBay. Claire Vo July 5, 2026 · 8 min read In this episode of How I AI, I was so excited to sit down with Alessio Fanelli, the founder of Kernel Labs , a partner at Decibel Partners , and the co-host of one of my favorite podcasts, Latent Space . Alessio is one of those builders who is truly living in the future, and he shared a couple of workflows that perfectly illustrate the shift from simply prompting an AI to actually managing a team of autonomous agents. We often hear people talk about orchestrating agents, but it's rare to see a practical, working system. Most people are still very much human-in-the-loop, guiding every step. Alessio, however, has moved past that. He's become an "agent manager," overseeing his AI workforce from his phone using standard project management tools. It’s a completely different way of thinking about productivity. He walked us through two fascinating use cases that are worlds apart. First, he showed me his "software factory," an autonomous coding setup using OpenAI's Symphony , Linear , and a cloud server to handle his engineering tasks. Then, for something completely different, he demonstrated how he uses an AI agent to go shopping for underpriced, high-value Pokémon cards for his store, Merlin Games .
